commitments.text: legislating a new target of net-zero carbon emissions by 2050
reducing GHG emissions to 80-95% below 1990 levels by 2050

measurability.reason: All commitments have specific numbers, units, target years, and baselines.

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC commitments lack quantification and specificity compared to IPCC recommendations.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: reducing emissions to net-zero by 2050 (NDC2_2021_Canada, p.1)
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: global net zero CO2 by 2050 required for 1.5°C (WG3_SPM_2022, p.31)

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC commitment of 80-95% reduction below 1990 levels by 2050 is inconsistent with IPCC recommendations for a 1.5°C pathway.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: reducing GHG emissions to 80-95% below 1990 levels by 2050 (NDC2_2021_Canada, p.28)
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: global net zero CO2 by 2050 required for 1.5°C (WG3_SPM_2022, p.31)

gap_findings.description: Canada's NDC commitment is unconditional, but some countries' commitments are conditional on international finance.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: reducing emissions to net-zero by 2050 (NDC2_2021_Canada, p.1)
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: some countries' commitments are conditional on international finance (WG3_Chapter04_MitigationDevelopmentPathways_2022, p.24)

details.summary: Canada's NDC commitments lack specificity and quantification, and are inconsistent with IPCC recommendations for a 1.5°C pathway.

pass_c.country_summary: Canada's NDC commitments lack specificity and quantification, and are inconsistent with IPCC recommendations for a 1.5°C pathway.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Canada should strengthen its NDC commitment to align with IPCC recommendations for a 1.5°C pathway, and provide more specific and quantifiable targets.
